_meta.description: Curated MITRE ATT&CK techniques for the defensive OSINT framing (master plan Part 3.4 / 4.1). Emphasis on TA0043 Reconnaissance and TA0042 Resource Development — 'here is what an adversary could learn about you from open sources'.

techniques.name: Active Scanning
techniques.text: Adversaries scan victim infrastructure to gather information for targeting. Passive exposure: open services and versions visible in public internet-scan data (Shodan/Censys) reveal attack surface without the adversary ever touching your systems.
techniques.defensive_note: Minimize the public service/version footprint; monitor what internet-scan datasets already expose about you.

techniques.name: Gather Victim Network Information
techniques.text: Adversaries gather network information such as IP ranges, DNS records and domain properties. DNS, WHOIS/RDAP and certificate-transparency logs disclose subdomains, name servers and resolved IPs from entirely public sources.
techniques.defensive_note: Audit DNS/WHOIS/CT exposure; retire stale subdomains discoverable via certificate transparency.

techniques.name: Gather Victim Host Information
techniques.text: Adversaries gather host details — software, configurations and versions. HTTP response headers and fingerprints reveal web server, framework and CMS versions that map to known CVEs.
techniques.defensive_note: Suppress version-disclosing headers; keep public-facing technology patched.

techniques.name: Gather Victim Identity Information
techniques.text: Adversaries gather identity data such as email addresses and employee names. Email-pattern inference and breach-corpus lookups expose valid address formats useful for phishing.
techniques.defensive_note: Enforce MFA and phishing-resistant auth; monitor breach corpora for exposed corporate credentials.

techniques.name: Search Open Websites/Domains
techniques.text: Adversaries search freely available websites and domains for victim information, including social media and public code repositories.
techniques.defensive_note: Govern what is published publicly; scan public repos for leaked secrets and internal references.

techniques.name: Search Open Technical Databases
techniques.text: Adversaries search public technical databases — DNS/WHOIS, certificate transparency, scan repositories and CDNs — for victim infrastructure.
techniques.defensive_note: Treat CT logs, WHOIS and scan datasets as adversary intelligence sources; reduce what they reveal.

techniques.name: Network Service Discovery
techniques.text: Active scanning enumerates listening services and ports on a target host. Each open port is a live service an adversary would probe next; banners disclose product and version that map to known CVEs. This is the authorized active-recon output — discovery only, never exploitation.
techniques.defensive_note: Close or firewall unnecessary ports; restrict management services to VPN; suppress version banners.

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.text: An adversary's likely NEXT step after recon: exploiting a weakness in an internet-facing service or web endpoint discovered during scanning/crawling. HORUS Sentinel maps this as a downstream risk to prioritize — it performs discovery only and stops here, before any exploitation.
techniques.defensive_note: Patch and harden internet-facing apps; put a WAF in front; reduce the exposed endpoint/form surface found by the crawler.

techniques.name: External Remote Services
techniques.text: Exposed remote-access services (RDP 3389, SSH 22, VPN, databases) discovered by active scanning are prime adversary targets for later access. Their presence on the public internet is a high-priority exposure finding.
techniques.defensive_note: Place remote-access behind VPN/MFA; never expose databases (3306/5432/6379/27017/9200) to the internet.

techniques.name: Acquire Infrastructure
techniques.text: Adversaries acquire infrastructure — domains, servers, botnets — to stage operations. Look-alike domains and newly registered infrastructure signal pre-attack staging.
techniques.defensive_note: Monitor for look-alike/typosquat domains and newly registered infrastructure referencing your brand.

techniques.name: Compromise Infrastructure
techniques.text: Adversaries compromise third-party infrastructure to use during targeting. Poor-reputation IPs in your resolution chain or supply chain raise risk.
techniques.defensive_note: Enrich resolved IPs with reputation feeds; alert on adjacency to known-malicious infrastructure.

geo_taxonomy.name: Regional Instability Indicator
geo_taxonomy.text: Composite instability derived from historical event density (GTD/GDELT). High instability regions show sustained armed-assault and IED modalities against military, police and government targets.

geo_taxonomy.name: Dominant Attack Modality
geo_taxonomy.text: The prevailing tactic in a region-year — e.g. IED, armed assault, bombing/explosion — indicating the most probable threat pattern for defensive posture.
